behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths, API names (RenderAbstractViewport.maybeOf, Scrollable.maybeOf, isExpanding, _CollapsibleBody, SizeTransition, FadeTransition, Alignment.topCenter, EntryDatetimeWidget) asserted by tests are named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction literally prescribes the implementation: names the variable `final isExpanding`, specifies `RenderAbstractViewport.maybeOf`, `Scrollable.maybeOf`, `_CollapsibleBody` StatefulWidget, `SizeTransition`/`FadeTransition` with `sizeFactor`/`axisAlignment`, `Alignment.topCenter`, and exact steps like 'Remove the datePadding variable'.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific names like `final isExpanding`, `_CollapsibleBody`, `RenderAbstractViewport.maybeOf` — a correct alternative fix (e.g., using a different variable name or different viewport API) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The f2p tests (test_scroll_uses_viewport_check, test_header_shows_date_when_expanded, etc.) are all grep-style string searches on source files rather than executing code or widget tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific code constructs to be present; a stub returning defaults couldn't satisfy the string/structural checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each of the 5 expected behaviors (conditional scroll, expanded header date, SizeTransition body, top alignment, no duplicate date) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates/pass_to_pass, Fail-to-pass) and named descriptively.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is UI code, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and symbols in instruction.md match the repo structure and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names exact variable names (`isExpanding`, `datePadding`), exact private class names (`_CollapsibleBody`/`_CollapsibleBodyState`), and exact APIs (`RenderAbstractViewport.maybeOf`, `Scrollable.maybeOf`, `SizeTransition`, `Alignment.topCenter`) — this is implementation dictation rather than problem description.
